title: I'm But A Stranger Here

metadata.composer: 'St. Edmund (Sullivan)' Arthur Seymour Sullivan, 1872 "Church Praise, with Tunes", 1885
metadata.copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2011 Revision.
c_lines: Music: 'St. Edmund (Sullivan)' Arthur Seymour Sullivan, 1872.  Setting: "Church Praise, with Tunes", 1885.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2011 Revision.

1.raw_text: I'm but a stranger here, Heav'n is my home; Earth is a desert drear, Heav'n is my home. Danger and sorrow stand round me on every hand; Heav'n is my fatherland, Heav'n is my home.

2.raw_text: What though the tempest rage, Heav'n is my home; Short is my pilgrimage, Heav'n is my home; Time's wild and wintry blast soon shall be over past; I shall reach home at last, Heav'n is my home.

3.raw_text: There at my Savior's side Heav'n is my home; I shall be glori- fied, Heav'n is my home. There are the good and blest, those I loved most and best; There, too, I soon shall rest, Heav'n is my home.

4.raw_text: Therefore I murmur not, Heav'n is my home; Whate'er my earthly lot, Heav'n is my home; And I shall surely stand there at my Lord's right hand. Heav'n is my fatherland, Heav'n is my home.
